architecture_gap: Recursive evolution stalls if any one of runtime stability, registry intelligence, or event-sourced replay is missing.
data_model_changes: Add cycle history, meta-evaluation lineage, and merged improvement tracks to canonical state.
governance_implications: Meta-evaluation becomes a first-class recurrent governance step.
invention_concept: Merge Alpha service hardening, Beta registry/task-graph autonomy, and Gamma event-sourced recursive evolution.
migration_strategy: Carry forward all existing runtime artifacts while promoting the merged proposal into the next CSSM version.
problem_statement: No single swarm objective is sufficient; the AAS must combine stability, capability, and long-horizon evolution simultaneously.
proposed_architecture_change: Synthesize a new canonical CSSM version that adopts the strongest elements from all three swarms.
protocol_implications: The AAS evolves via explicit, repeatable, proposal-driven cycles rather than one-shot bootstrap logic.
security_implications: Replayable cycle history improves auditability and recovery confidence.
title: Cycle 011 Meta-Merge Architecture
